steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both texts criticize scholars for fixating on the fragmentation-versus-coherence debate in modernist fiction at the expense of other stylistic elements. The correct answer must reflect that shared concern about this narrow focus distorting the picture of the period.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'scholars should expand their focus beyond stylistic analysis entirely': goes further than either text argues — both stay within stylistic analysis. ✗
  • Choice B — 'without a major shift in focus, the presented vision of modernist fiction will remain unnecessarily limited': directly captures both texts' warning that the fragmentation-versus-coherence fixation narrows understanding. ✓
  • Choice C — 'current analytical methods for studying modernist fiction are obsolete': neither text calls the methods obsolete, only the singular focus. ✗
  • Choice D — 'some modernist authors deserve rehabilitation of their critical reputations': off-topic — neither text discusses individual authors' reputations. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
